133534.explanation: The municipality of Krimpenerwaard has the lowest municipal taxes in the region. Gemeente Belang wants to keep it that way. A slight increase could be acceptable if it helps preserve social facilities in our centers.

133535.explanation: An increase to the national average yields relatively little extra income, while there is a risk that the number of overnight stays will decrease. Tourists spend money on hospitality, shops, museums, and activities. There should be no high barrier to staying overnight.

133536.explanation: We notice a great need for more business parks for our (local) entrepreneurs. This can partly be achieved by revitalizing existing business parks, but new business parks are also necessary.

133537.explanation: The municipality must set opening hours for cafes and restaurants to prevent (noise) nuisance and to guarantee safety as much as possible.

133538.explanation: Shops may already be open 6 days a week between 7:00 and 23:00. Entrepreneurs must have the freedom to open on Sundays. They always decide for themselves whether to use this freedom.

133539.explanation: Gemeente Belang considers nature development important. In recent years, 2250 hectares of farmland have already been converted to nature reserves. As far as Gemeente Belang is concerned, this is sufficient for now. Voluntary conversion remains a possibility.

133540.explanation: The municipality should not want to determine policy on this itself. The influx of refugees is primarily a national issue. The municipality will have to follow national regulations and not develop its own policy here.

133542.explanation: Allowing small-scale housing construction on agricultural plots can create additional homes without large-scale new development projects or damaging open spaces. It often involves one or a few houses, fitting the landscape and existing infrastructure.

133545.explanation: Property tax (OZB) must be paid by everyone, including associations. There are many subsidy schemes that associations can apply for to compensate for their activities.

133546.explanation: The municipality already has several special enforcement officers (BOA's) who focus on our safety. We must continue to look at an effective collaboration with the police to ensure the process runs as efficiently as possible.

133547.explanation: Gemeente Belang is not in favor of legalizing soft drugs due to the nuisance this can cause. This approach aims to prevent so-called drug tourism.

133548.explanation: Camera surveillance can be used where necessary. According to VGBK, it does not need to be expanded as a standard measure.

133549.explanation: It is important to allocate a significant portion of new homes for social rental housing. The housing vision adopted by the municipal council forms the basis for this.

133551.explanation: The municipality should not automatically raise the income threshold. This merely shifts the problem to another target group. Structural poverty reduction requires national income policies: higher wages, better benefits, and fair tax measures that strengthen purchasing power.

133552.explanation: Residents collectively own many cars. However, there is insufficient space for everyone to park in front of their own door. Better use should also be made of the already available parking spaces within each village. Gemeente Belang has a positive stance towards expansion where possible.

133553.explanation: Our villages must also be supplied in time. Gemeente Belang sees environmental zones as a major restriction. However, we should look at measures to reduce nuisance caused by heavy traffic in some villages.

133554.explanation: The number of electric cars is expected to increase significantly in the coming years. This requires a gradual increase in the number of charging points. The municipality should install additional charging places only when there is actual demand in the specific street or neighborhood, and not designate them where there is (yet) no demand.

133555.explanation: Preferably yes, but in practice, this should come as much as possible from the residents and associations themselves. This is not yet successful in every village. The municipality must also work towards a fair distribution of available resources, taking local situations into account.

133556.explanation: It is good to keep free resources available for people who have difficulty acquiring them themselves. These free items should be available in as many public buildings as possible, such as libraries and community centers.

133557.explanation: Where possible and appropriate, companies should be given space to expand even at existing industrial sites. Not all industrial sites have room around them, so in practice this will concern only a few suitable locations. Preferably for local entrepreneurs.

133558.explanation: Nearly all villages in Krimpenerwaard have a library branch. Existing small libraries must be able to meet all new requirements set for libraries. Therefore, it is preferable to invest in the current branches rather than creating more libraries.

133559.explanation: We choose to deploy the available hours of our special enforcement officers on the other six days. On Sundays, the police are available for all reports.

133560.explanation: We want every village to receive attention, not just the one with the most active advisory board. The municipal council fulfills this democratic role. Residents can already participate in discussions regarding plans in a village, which helps in making the right choices.

133561.explanation: Our party is against citizens' assemblies because decisions should be made by elected representatives (municipal council). Citizens' assemblies lack democratic legitimacy and can lead to arbitrariness. Responsibility must remain with the voters.

133562.explanation: It is important for the municipality to convey that everyone may be themselves! It is not necessary to do this in every village. It suffices to do this at a few municipal buildings and to raise awareness about it online.

133563.explanation: This helps reduce the distance between residents and the executive board (B&W). It is a low-threshold form of contact.

133564.explanation: The Silver Museum has a long-term subsidy in which it has been agreed that the subsidy will gradually decrease in the coming years. Therefore, there will be no further increase in the subsidy to the Silver Museum. The museum must continue its activities within the currently available subsidy.

133565.explanation: This is not a municipal task. Currently, entrepreneurs can already open entertainment venues, including those specifically for youth. Unfortunately, few entrepreneurs actually take this up in practice.

133567.explanation: Only if the terrace is located on municipal land. Currently, some entrepreneurs do not pay fees but do use public space for their terrace. Entrepreneurs should rent this space or pay a tax for it.

133568.explanation: Burial in Krimpenerwaard is costly. There is little space available and establishing new cemeteries is expensive due to the soil conditions. Gemeente Belang believes these costs should be borne more by those who choose burial, at least more than is currently the case.

133569.explanation: There may be local situations that require the municipality to impose rules. This should be done carefully and preferably only when absolutely necessary.

133570.explanation: All residents, associations, and businesses must be involved equally when the situation calls for it.

133571.explanation: This must be discussed per location and depends on many factors. Sometimes a location is only temporarily available and a shorter period can be chosen. It is important that the surrounding area knows the agreements made and that these agreements are honored at the end of the period.

133572.explanation: The number of electric cars is expected to increase significantly in the coming years. This requires a gradual increase in charging points. However, this should not happen too quickly; no one benefits from unused parking spaces.

133573.explanation: A complete ban goes too far for Gemeente Belang. It is good, however, to work with sports clubs to review the times at which alcohol may be served. For example, not during the day but only in the evening.

133574.explanation: It is a law, so the municipality must in principle comply. However, no suitable locations for large-scale asylum seeker reception have been found in Krimpenerwaard. In practice, we cannot comply with this law.

133575.explanation: New provincial policy will probably allow farmyard wind turbines (small turbines at farms or businesses) to reach a maximum height of 30 meters in 2026. Gemeente Belang wants to incorporate this increase into local policy as well.

133576.explanation: Medium and high wind turbines do not belong in the open landscape of Krimpenerwaard. This is to preserve the open landscape and also to protect (meadow) birds.

133577.explanation: This reduces accessibility for people with mobility impairments or wheelchair users. People with strollers or carts will also experience inconvenience. It is better to invest in high-quality existing greenery and sufficient maintenance, where there is still room for improvement.

133578.explanation: Under certain conditions, this should be possible, with early participation of residents being very important.

133580.explanation: The province has designated several locations as highly valuable for meadow birds. Meadow birds may also nest at new construction sites, which will be revealed by research. The municipal council will always take this into account when making decisions.

133581.explanation: Allowing housing on agricultural plots can create additional homes. It is important that such plans fit with the landscape and existing infrastructure.

133582.explanation: Agreements with surrounding ambulance posts ensure that on the north and west sides of Krimpenerwaard, the nearest ambulance will always respond to a call. A municipal contribution is not a guarantee for better response times.

133584.explanation: Although the current family coaches do excellent work, no additional deployment is needed. The current level of effort is sufficient for now, in our view.

133585.explanation: That is a great way to ensure that children can continue participating, even if their financial situation at home does not allow it. There is already a scheme where the municipality contributes to a computer or laptop purchase through a store. A PC or laptop can also be provided via the school as a supplement.

133586.explanation: The current scheme for free public transport for people with low incomes is sufficient. Many people aged 67 or older can afford these costs themselves.

133587.explanation: The current operating hours until midnight (0:00) are sufficient.

133588.explanation: Only when there are no other options can a parking garage be considered. Existing parking spaces should first be better utilized, even if this means a few minutes' walk.

133590.explanation: The Bastille itself also has an important role in this. Of course, the municipality can support the search and possibly provide a small financial contribution. However, the organization itself must also generate its own income.

133592.explanation: Good information helps to increase vaccination willingness. It remains important to ensure that the vaccination rate in Krimpenerwaard does not decline further.

133593.explanation: The current scheme for free public transport for people with low incomes is sufficient. There are many young people whose parents can easily pay these costs themselves. This should not be charged to the municipality. Some of these young people also have a student public transport pass.

133534.explanation: The Property Tax (OZB) has been kept at the same level for 10 years by first critically reviewing internal expenses, and the municipality must continue to do so. Watch closely for unnecessary policies. If new policies are introduced, the old ones must be cleaned up first. According to the SGP, the OZB may therefore only increase for very targeted investments (in facilities).

133535.explanation: Tourists and the SGP actually find it quite normal to pay for the many facilities that Krimpenerwaard offers, such as the 7 swimming pools. If tourists do not pay, the residents will have to bear the costs.

133536.explanation: To remain attractive for business as a municipality, we will need to offer more space. Existing companies also want to expand, and as SGP, we do not like to see them leave for that reason.

133537.explanation: We find it very undesirable if cafés and the like are open at the most unusual times and partygoers cause nuisance to the surroundings; imagine living next door. As SGP, we would not like to take on that responsibility.

133538.explanation: There is no reason for the SGP or entrepreneurs to extend store opening hours. Stores can be open six days a week, they will not necessarily sell more if the days are extended or Sundays are included. Consider also the staff who occasionally want to have a day off.

133539.explanation: Krimpenerwaard has had to give up over 2,000 hectares of farmland in favor of nature; we as SGP members believe we have more than done our part. Besides, there is no nature more beautiful than farmland with cows and sheep, the polder ditches, and pollard willows.

133540.explanation: Krimpenerwaard has virtually no available housing due to the Province's strict building policy. The few houses that do become available are just as urgently needed for our own starters. Building must be possible first before there is anything to choose from, says the SGP.

133542.explanation: The SGP is eager to build houses and does everything to achieve that. But it is undesirable if houses appear randomly in the countryside and spoil the appearance of our polder. Therefore, the municipality must look with landowners for good places to build, mainly on the edges of centers.

133545.explanation: Every homeowner and every association that has a clubhouse must contribute to the costs. The SGP believes sports clubs should become more self-reliant. Vital clubs can achieve a lot through sponsorship. There are various excellent examples of this.

133546.explanation: Comparisons show that at most one additional enforcer could be hired. Fortunately, Krimpenerwaard is a very safe municipality, so the necessity is not very high, but the costs are. The SGP wants to ensure that the enforcement officers (BOA) do not have to work on Sundays; they deserve a day off as well.

133547.explanation: No one believes coffee shops have a good influence on public health. On the contrary, the SGP shudders at the mere idea. You would not want such nuisance locations in any center.

133548.explanation: Krimpenerwaard is a safe municipality; for the SGP, surveillance cameras are way too far. If more cameras are necessary, then cameras along roads that flash for speeding and noise violations.

133549.explanation: Krimpenerwaard is moving towards the legal norm of 30% social rental housing, which is sufficient. The SGP prefers homeownership; for the immediate living environment, this also gives a nice boost in appearance.

133551.explanation: Krimpenerwaard currently sets the limit for assistance at 120% or 130% of the minimum income. That is a good national standard. If the limit is extended to 140%, the incentive for a new group of residents to generate more income disappears. The SGP is in favor of activation towards work.

133552.explanation: In several centers, the parking problem is substantial, but there is no room in the centers for additional parking spaces. Therefore, the SGP wants to look for extra spaces on the edges of some larger centers. This means not all four car owners can park at their doorstep but must walk a short distance.

133553.explanation: As SGP, we find it very far-fetched to deny access to suppliers and market traders who only drive in the center for a few minutes. Traffic on highways and in the air causes much more pollution; tackle that first.

133554.explanation: Of course, the SGP supports enough charging points; however, the parking problem in various centers is currently possibly even bigger. For every new charging point, a parking space disappears. Therefore, at this moment, we prioritize parking over charging in every street.

133555.explanation: In principle, almost every center has a place where residents can meet, which is very important. However, some cultural centers cost the municipality a lot of money, so the SGP is cautious about expanding this further at taxpayers' expense.

133556.explanation: If there was apparently already a need for this facility, then we as SGP do not see it as a municipal task. The costs are then many times higher than the material itself. Needs can also be met through vending machines in supermarkets.

133557.explanation: The SGP sees that companies are outgrowing their spaces and are eager to expand. Currently, we can hardly offer these companies space. If the strict provincial policy could be somewhat relaxed, we could provide our entrepreneurs with the necessary premises.

133558.explanation: Almost all centers already have a library facility. This makes books accessible to everyone, which is very important for the SGP. Additionally, we want to keep costs under control.

133559.explanation: For the SGP, this is a bad plan for three reasons: there is no safety problem, the costs will rise significantly, and our enforcers also want to go to church and visit family. They deserve a wonderful day of rest as well.

133560.explanation: Advisory councils like those already existing on specific topics such as the Social Domain are very important to the SGP. But an advisory council for each center creates (too) high expectations of the municipal council, which must make the final decisions. Let's avoid disappointments.

133561.explanation: As SGP, we prefer to focus on information meetings and citizen surveys and panels. That already provides a lot of information for the municipality. Ultimately, the municipal council is mandated for four years to make wise decisions. Every election features a prime example of citizen deliberation.

133562.explanation: The government must remain neutral, according to the SGP. If the flag is raised in every center, certain population groups are prominently celebrated at the expense of other groups who watch this happen with regret.

133563.explanation: It is fine if every center has recognizable representatives and thus offers a contact point. That is also why the SGP is present every year at all fairs and is often used as a point of contact.

133564.explanation: The SGP has always advocated for more sponsorship by businesses of the Silver Museum. Now taxpayers pay for every admission ticket.

133565.explanation: The SGP thinks entrepreneurs already have the opportunity to open nightlife venues. However, today's youth prefer going out in the cities. Several national youth venues have closed in recent years for this reason. It is definitely not the municipality's task to finance this.

133567.explanation: Sometimes hospitality businesses occupy the entire terrace and half the street; in those situations, the SGP finds it quite conceivable to ask for a reasonable contribution from these commercial hospitality businesses.

133568.explanation: Burial is already very expensive in Krimpenerwaard. That is why the SGP has been concerned about burial fees for 10 years. With success, by cutting costs, the fees have remained at the current level.

133569.explanation: Local government should facilitate as much as possible and not obstruct. Nevertheless, sometimes typical local rules are needed to prevent problems; in those cases, the SGP weighs pros and cons as critically as possible against each other.

133570.explanation: There is a lot of (local) knowledge among entrepreneurs; as SGP, we think the municipality should take advantage of this. By the way, this is already common practice in many situations.

133571.explanation: The SGP thinks it is good if new situations come to light after three years. On the one hand for neighbors, and on the other hand, it would not be good if asylum seekers had to leave after 15 years.

133572.explanation: Yes, the SGP thinks more charging points should be installed since electric car use will increase again in the future. However, these places must not come at the expense of parking spaces in centers where there is already intense parking pressure.

133573.explanation: Sport and alcohol do not go together at all. The SGP greatly appreciates being sporty and exercising. It is very strange that sports canteens simultaneously earn most of their income from alcohol sales.

133574.explanation: Krimpenerwaard has contributed its share to asylum reception. There simply are no more reception places. Some municipalities provide far less reception, and they should be accommodated first. That is the SGP's view.

133575.explanation: It is quite conceivable that small mills on farmyards for their own power supply may be allowed to be higher than 15 meters. We as SGP want to facilitate that, especially if the mill is kept as much as possible out of direct sight.

133576.explanation: Wind turbines ranging from 100 to even 240 meters do not belong in the unique landscape of the Krimpenerwaard according to the SGP. They do not look out of place at sea, but in our polders cows and sheep belong. No wind turbines that also have an extremely negative impact on bird populations.

133577.explanation: The municipality must work more towards a climate-adaptive situation. This means that excess rainfall can be absorbed much better by the soil. And that overheating of neighborhoods is mitigated by more greenery. For the SGP, this should only happen when the street is being renovated, to save costs.

133578.explanation: Solar on land contributes only a limited amount to the energy transition and also creates an enormous ugly image in the landscape. As SGP, we prefer to first fully cover all large roofs. Perhaps after that, solar panels can be installed on land unsuitable for agriculture.

133580.explanation: If the SGP has to choose between, on the one hand, the departure of our youth from our cores due to housing shortage and, on the other hand, the departure of meadow birds to other parcels, it is clear to us where the priority lies.

133581.explanation: Every existing neighborhood was once built on agricultural land. The farmer in question has been generously compensated, and the severe housing shortage is addressed this way. For the SGP, it is clear based on these principles that building takes priority.

133582.explanation: The municipality is not responsible for ambulance posts; these are determined by the Safety Region and the response times to accidents. For the SGP, however, short response times are so important that we want to make an exception in this case and consult about a possible contribution to costs.

133584.explanation: The municipality already successfully uses certain professional methods to help prevent separations of parents, for example. It is known that the sad fact of a separation is the largest cost item in Youth Care. This investment is worth it; for the children!

133585.explanation: The municipality has various schemes to support families with little money. For the SGP, this does not need to be immediately expanded. Parents can help their children keep up at school, for example, with a second-hand device.

133586.explanation: The problem is not so much that elderly people cannot afford public transport. For the SGP, it is especially important that all villages are well accessible by public transport.

133587.explanation: By extending sailing times at night, the ferry service must deploy an extra night crew. That means a highly unprofitable service during the nighttime hours, with the costs entirely borne by the taxpayer. While a 10-minute detour via the Viaanse bridge offers a solution.

133588.explanation: Parking garages are generally not beauties, thinks the SGP, and cost an incredible amount of money. Maybe we can also walk a few minutes together to places at the edges of the center where there is almost always sufficient parking space.

133590.explanation: The municipality can only spend the money once; if every group claims free housing and beer, the property tax will not rise by 2.5% but by 25% each year. For the SGP it is clear that this does not immediately belong to the municipality's core tasks.

133592.explanation: Of course, vaccination information is fine, but the municipality should not go so far in its messages that people feel forced. It remains a free choice also made by more and more highly educated parents, not only on grounds of faith objections. For the SGP, no coercion.

133593.explanation: Money is not so much the problem for young people; public transport connections are a bigger concern for the SGP, and we want to work on that. Besides, young people are sporty enough to cycle some distance against the wind.

133534.explanation: Leefbaar opposes structural tax increases. Only an inflation correction can be discussed to keep existing provisions affordable, not for new policies or growth of government.

133535.explanation: Visitors use facilities and public space. A tourist tax around the national average is reasonable, so that costs for maintenance, recreation, and livability do not fall entirely on our residents.

133536.explanation: Strengthening employment and the local economy requires sufficient space for businesses, appropriate to accessibility and surroundings. Infill development of existing business parks has priority. New business parks must be carefully integrated, respecting landscape and living environment.

133537.explanation: Café and restaurant owners themselves know best what suits their business and surroundings. The municipality should allow space, with enforcement focused on nuisance rather than strict closing times.

133538.explanation: Shopkeepers should decide themselves when they are open, including Sundays. Sunday opening is allowed but not mandatory. This preserves entrepreneurs’ freedom of choice and keeps villages and centers lively.

133539.explanation: Agriculture and nature are both of great value for the Krimpenerwaard. One must not come at the expense of the other. Forced conversion of agricultural land is undesirable, but voluntary and area-oriented solutions should remain possible.

133540.explanation: Status holders do not get priority for social rental housing. They join the same waiting list as every other home seeker. Scarce homes must be distributed fairly, with equal rules for everyone.

133542.explanation: Farmers should be given the possibility to build homes on their own yard or land, provided this is small-scale and appropriate (no residential neighborhoods). This helps the housing shortage, supports business succession, and keeps the countryside livable.

133545.explanation: Sports clubs are indispensable for health, social contact, and youth. Clubs should be exempted from property tax so that fees remain affordable and facilities are preserved.

133546.explanation: More visible enforcement officers increase safety and livability in the Krimpenerwaard. Extra enforcement officers enable targeted enforcement on nuisance, traffic, and public order, also in evenings and weekends, including Sundays!

133547.explanation: Coffeeshops do not fit the character of the Krimpenerwaard. The municipality should focus on safety and combating drug use and trade, not on facilitating.

133548.explanation: Camera surveillance at strategic and nuisance-sensitive locations helps prevent crime and supports investigations. It increases residents’ feeling of safety and is used carefully with respect for privacy.

133549.explanation: An accessible housing market requires sufficient affordable homes. Including at least 35% social rental housing in new construction gives starters and people with lower incomes more chance of a home in their own municipality.

133551.explanation: By raising the income limit, the municipality can reach more residents who need financial support. The goal remains to help as many people as possible out of poverty and structurally reduce existential insecurity.

133552.explanation: Residents must be able to park in their own neighborhood. By creating extra parking spaces and structurally tackling parking pressure, villages remain accessible, livable, and welcoming for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs.

133553.explanation: Leefbaar opposes the introduction of an environmental zone. Destination traffic must always remain possible for entrepreneurs and residents. Nuisance from heavy traffic should be addressed per village or city with detours and enforcement.

133554.explanation: More charging facilities are needed and desired, but must not come at the expense of regular parking spaces. Residents must be able to keep parking in their own neighborhood, with smart solutions for charging without extra parking pressure.

133555.explanation: Every village must have an accessible cultural and/or community center for social and cultural activities. Such centers strengthen cohesion, provide space for associations, and make support nearby and accessible.

133556.explanation: Menstrual products are basic necessities. The municipality must prevent shame or lack of money from leading to exclusion. This contributes to combating poverty.

133557.explanation: First, we use existing business parks to prevent vacancy. Where necessary, expansion is possible to give entrepreneurs space and create employment. Development must be carefully integrated, with attention to surroundings and livability.

133558.explanation: The focus is on good, accessible library facilities and service points in villages and towns. Expanding the number of libraries is not necessary if existing facilities are accessible and well-maintained.

133559.explanation: Nuisance and crime do not stop on Sundays. That is why enforcement officers must also be deployed on weekends, so enforcement remains visible and residents can count on safety every day of the week.

133560.explanation: Every village must have its own advisory council where residents can think along about plans and developments. This better utilizes local knowledge and aligns policy better with what lives in each village.

133561.explanation: For major decisions, the municipality must actively involve residents through a citizens’ assembly. This strengthens support, increases trust in politics, and improves decision-making quality.

133562.explanation: Acceptance and respect are self-evident, but mandatory symbolic displays do not necessarily contribute. Inclusion is achieved through policy and behavior, not through compulsory flag waving. We raise the flag centrally at the new town hall.

133563.explanation: Every village deserves a clear point of contact within the council. This ensures short lines of communication, better accessibility, and a greater sense of close governance.

133564.explanation: Culture is important, but subsidies must be carefully considered. Additional funds are only justifiable if they fit within the municipality's priorities and financial capacity.

133565.explanation: Entrepreneurs should have the space to open nightlife venues for young people. By having sufficient options within the municipality, we prevent youth from having to go to Rotterdam, Gouda, or Utrecht.

133567.explanation: The Krimpenerwaard currently has very few terraces. Additional taxes discourage entrepreneurs and harm vibrancy and social interaction. Terraces actually contribute to an attractive village and city center and should not be additionally taxed.

133568.explanation: The costs associated with burial must remain affordable. Increasing grave fees affects residents at a sensitive time and is socially undesirable.

133569.explanation: Entrepreneurs already face a pile-up of regulations that stifle business. The municipality should not impose additional local rules on top of national legislation and should instead provide space for entrepreneurship.

133570.explanation: Entrepreneurs form the foundation of the local community. They know the reality and directly feel the effects of policies. Actively involving them in plans leads to more realistic policies with greater support.

133571.explanation: Leefbaar opposes asylum seeker accommodation locations in the Krimpenerwaard. If accommodation is imposed nevertheless, it must only be temporary. No extensions and no permanent locations: after a maximum of three years, accommodation must definitely end.

133572.explanation: It should be easy for residents to charge their cars. More charging facilities are needed, but this must not come at the expense of regular parking spaces. Charging must be integrated practically without extra parking pressure.

133573.explanation: Sports canteens rely on the sale of drinks. A ban has financial consequences for clubs and harms social cohesion. Clubs can manage this responsibly themselves without additional municipal rules.

133574.explanation: Leefbaar is against accommodating asylum seekers in the Krimpenerwaard. The municipality must resist imposed quotas and take no steps toward accommodation locations, regardless of pressure from the national government.

133575.explanation: Leefbaar opposes wind turbines in the Krimpenerwaard. They do not fit into the open polder landscape, harm livability and views, and do not belong in our area.

133576.explanation: Leefbaar opposes all tall wind turbines. These do not belong in the Krimpenerwaard, disturb the landscape, and detract from livability and the character of our villages.

133577.explanation: The Krimpenerwaard is naturally green. By restoring more greenery in villages and towns, we improve livability, cooling, and water retention. Less concrete and more greenery create a more pleasant and healthier living environment.

133578.explanation: Solar panels belong on roofs and existing buildings. The characteristic open landscape must be protected against large-scale solar fields.

133580.explanation: For housing construction, careful consideration must be given to where building is appropriate and where it is not. Meadow bird areas are vulnerable and deserve protection. New plans always require a good balance between housing, nature, and landscape.

133581.explanation: Housing development must remain possible where it is done carefully, on a small scale and appropriately, with respect for agriculture, landscape, and adjacent residential neighborhoods.

133582.explanation: An additional ambulance station is necessary to shorten response times and save lives. Safety must not depend on postal codes.

133584.explanation: Family coaches provide early support to families and contribute to stable and safe home situations. By detecting and addressing problems earlier, we prevent escalation and give children and parents more peace and perspective.

133585.explanation: Every child must be able to participate well at school. The municipality must prevent children from lagging behind due to poverty, but support must be targeted and appropriate. The form of assistance requires careful consideration.

133586.explanation: Free public transport increases the independence of elderly people, prevents social isolation, and makes it easier to participate in community life within the municipality.

133587.explanation: Extended boating hours on weekends improve accessibility and safety and enhance the vibrancy of Schoonhoven for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs.

133588.explanation: Where parking problems are structural, parking garages can be a solution. These must be well integrated into the surroundings. Underground garages are a good option to spare space and landscape.

133590.explanation: Young people have the right to a fixed, safe meeting place. The municipality must take responsibility to provide DE Bastille with a new, suitable location.

133592.explanation: Good and clear information helps residents make well-considered choices about their health, without coercion or obligations.

133593.explanation: Free public transport increases accessibility for young people and makes participation in education, sports, and social activities easier and more accessible.

133534.explanation: PK opposes an increase in property tax above the inflation correction. Increases raise burdens for residents without guarantees of better decisions. Solutions must be found within the existing budget, with clear priorities and efficiency.

133535.explanation: Raising the tourist tax to the national average is wise because visitors then fairly contribute to the costs of public spaces, infrastructure, and enforcement. Provided that the rate remains competitive and revenues are visibly spent on livability and tourism.

133536.explanation: There are already enough business parks, many of which provide little employment. Our scarce and vulnerable space should be used for housing. Therefore, we prefer relocating existing business parks to less vulnerable areas.

133537.explanation: The current regulation suffices because rules are needed to prevent nuisance, noise disturbance, and other problems for neighbors. Exemptions can always be requested.

133538.explanation: On Sundays, shopkeepers should be able to choose whether to open, for example from 10:00 a.m. until the same closing time as weekdays. Weekday opening hours are clear and well regulated, making it straightforward for everyone. No critical voices are known regarding this.

133539.explanation: Pro Krimpenerwaard wants farmers to work sustainably and preserve our rural areas. Nature and agriculture go hand in hand. We are committed to protecting the polder landscape and balancing nature and farming. Nature-inclusive farming.

133540.explanation: We believe everyone should have equal opportunities for social rental housing. Therefore, the regular allocation system must lead again, including the hardship clause. This way, we address the housing crisis fairly, humanely, and practically.

133542.explanation: It can lead to uncontrolled urbanization, loss of farmland, and conflicts with spatial planning, environmental regulations, and nature.

133545.explanation: Exemption from property tax supports local sports, promotes social participation, and strengthens social cohesion. However, the scheme must be transparent and objective to ensure equal treatment and legal clarity.

133546.explanation: More enforcement officers (boa’s) in the Krimpenerwaard increase visibility and the feeling of safety, enforce rules faster, prevent escalation, and relieve the police. Green boa’s enforce rules in rural areas. Together, this strengthens safety.

133547.explanation: Increase in drug use and addiction, neighborhood nuisance, health risks, negative influence on youth, possible rise in crime related to sales, social problems, and neighborhood conflicts. We support good education in schools about all forms of addiction.

133548.explanation: Camera surveillance has pros and cons: it can increase safety and help solve crimes, but reduces privacy, is costly, can generate distrust, and does not always address underlying safety issues.

133549.explanation: We are in favor because the municipality has built too few social rental homes in recent years. Housing is a fundamental right, and not everyone can buy a house. At least 35% social rental housing ensures affordable homes and a fair chance for everyone.

133551.explanation: Despite prosperity, half a million Dutch people still live in poverty, including children. By raising the income limit to 130%, more residents gain access to minimum benefit schemes, enabling better participation and reducing inequality and social problems.

133552.explanation: Many apartments have been built within the built-up area of the Krimpenerwaard without sufficient parking spaces. This leads to parking nuisance and unsafe situations. Additional parking spaces, according to current standards, are necessary for livability and accessibility.

133553.explanation: Do this where possible. An environmental zone reduces emissions, noise, and fine particles in villages, improves health and livability, and protects historic centers. It encourages cleaner transport and prevents unnecessarily heavy freight traffic on narrow village roads.

133554.explanation: More public charging spots and safe home charging (also without a driveway) are needed. Electric driving reduces CO₂ emissions, ensures cleaner air, and less noise pollution. This improves public health, lowers costs, and makes sustainable transport accessible to everyone.

133555.explanation: Every village having its own community center means social gatherings, fun activities, and culture close to home. This way, people get to know each other better, a sense of community arises, and everyone feels more at home in their own neighborhood.

133556.explanation: Everyone should be able to get free tampons and sanitary pads, so no one gets into trouble or feels ashamed due to lack of money. This contributes to equal opportunities, health, and self-confidence for all residents of the municipality.

133557.explanation: Krimpenerwaard must not allow expansion of existing industrial sites. Our party believes this leads to unnecessary use of space, landscape degradation, and pressure on infrastructure. Existing sites can be used more efficiently without new expansions.

133558.explanation: A library in every center stimulates reading, learning, and social encounters. This ensures everyone has easy access to knowledge, information, and cultural activities, young and old, which increases livability and community involvement.

133559.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) must also work on Sundays in Krimpenerwaard due to, among other things, increased recreational pressure. Their presence prevents nuisance, enforces rules, and ensures equal safety every day, so supervision remains effective and livability for residents and visitors is guaranteed.

133560.explanation: Major topics are first discussed with residents of the centers. This reduces the distance between the municipality and residents and creates better support. Therefore, each center deserves its own (residential) vision.

133561.explanation: Major topics are first discussed with residents of the centers. This reduces the distance between the municipality and residents and creates better support. Therefore, each center deserves its own (residential) vision.

133562.explanation: Visible rainbow flags show that everyone in Krimpenerwaard is welcome and safe. It increases acceptance, support for LGBTI+ residents and youth, and shows that the municipality stands for equality and human rights.

133563.explanation: One alderman per center is impractical: there are more centers than aldermen, which leads to fragmentation, extra administrative pressure, unclear responsibilities, and slower decision-making. This harms cohesion and efficient governance.

133564.explanation: The Silver Museum receives an additional structural €300,000 per year on top of the previous sufficient level of €45,000. During times of budget cuts, we find this irresponsible. The museum should generate more own income and be freely accessible to residents, since it is funded with public money.

133565.explanation: We are positive about this. By simplifying rules and supporting entrepreneurs, a more attractive offer for young people emerges. This increases livability, keeps youth in the municipality, and simultaneously stimulates the local economy.

133567.explanation: Terraces use public space. It is reasonable that hospitality entrepreneurs provide a fair and proportional contribution for this, especially now that the municipality faces significant financial cuts.

133568.explanation: Increasing grave fees can provide the municipality with extra income for maintenance and expansion of cemeteries. However, it must not place too much financial pressure on residents. A balanced price increase, possibly with discounts for lower incomes, seems wise.

133569.explanation: Local regulations protect the environment, safety, and living conditions, provide tailor-made solutions, and prevent problems that national rules miss.

133570.explanation: That depends on the type of plans. Too much involvement can delay decision-making, cause conflicts of interest, and allow plans to be overly influenced by entrepreneurs. Therefore, combine this with a citizen council per center to also safeguard other social interests.

133571.explanation: A three-year limit prevents temporary shelters from becoming permanent and at the same time promotes social acceptance, since locations are not perceived as permanent solutions.

133572.explanation: The municipality can install more charging stations to make electric driving more attractive, reduce CO₂ emissions, and improve air quality. This encourages sustainability, promotes innovation, and supports residents switching to clean energy.

133573.explanation: Alcohol sales in sports canteens generate income and attract adults. A total ban limits freedom of choice, social atmosphere, and finances. However, restrictions on serving times can be considered.

133574.explanation: Quickly accommodating asylum seekers shows solidarity, complies with the law, and prevents sanctions. It can stimulate the local economy and employment, utilize vacant properties, and contribute to diversity and humanity. Distribution over the 11 centers is necessary.

133575.explanation: No wind turbines taller than 15 meters are allowed in Krimpenerwaard. No large wind turbines, but responsible small wind turbines on land and on high-rise buildings, with good protection for animals and nature.

133576.explanation: No wind turbines taller than 15 meters are allowed in Krimpenerwaard. No large wind turbines, but responsible small wind turbines on land and on high-rise buildings, with good protection for animals and nature.

133577.explanation: Replacing with greenery reduces heat stress, improves water drainage, increases biodiversity and livability. There are costs, but the benefits for climate adaptation and health are significant.

133578.explanation: Solar panels on the land damage landscape and nature, reduce agricultural land and biodiversity. Roofs and industrial sites offer sufficient space without land use. First choose solar on roofs, parking lots, etc., and efficient use of space.

133580.explanation: We oppose new construction in meadow bird areas because these birds depend on open, quiet landscapes. Buildings disturb their habitat, reduce breeding chances, and contribute to the disappearance of vulnerable species. The province supports us in this. There are sufficient other building locations.

133581.explanation: Caution with construction in rural areas is necessary because of water management, climate adaptation, CO₂ storage, public health, and landscape quality. Building is irreversible, nature fragmentation increases, and infrastructure is costly, while densification is more efficient.

133582.explanation: Ambulance care falls under the Regional Ambulance Provision and safety region; municipalities are not health insurers. Financing is through the healthcare system, not municipal budget. Co-financing could lead to unnecessary burdens for local taxpayers and shift national tasks to municipalities.

133584.explanation: Family coaches support families early, prevent escalation of problems and expensive youth care, strengthen parenting skills and social networks, and ensure children grow up safely and stably, which offers both human and financial benefits in the long term.

133585.explanation: In Krimpenerwaard, the municipal scheme for residents with low income is called the Participation Scheme, while families can turn to the Learning Money Foundation for school costs, a laptop, or a bike.

133586.explanation: Free public transport for those aged 67 and over increases their independence and participation in society. It helps reduce loneliness and makes facilities accessible.

133587.explanation: Provided that municipalities on the other side contribute to the extra costs. Advantages: stimulates going out and social contacts, safe alternative to car, promotes hospitality/tourism, more convenience for residents. Disadvantage: all extra costs are borne by the Krimpenerwaard municipality.

133588.explanation: Too few parking spots due to non-compliance with parking standards in new construction. Reduce parking pressure with free residents’ permits; visitors park elsewhere. Parking garages/higher parking decks offer a central, efficient solution. Alternative: better public transport and bicycle facilities.

133590.explanation: The municipality has demolished the old location and is responsible for a full alternative. Youth work is indispensable and proven effective. It offers young people a safe meeting place. There is hardly any youth center left in our villages.

133592.explanation: Broader education that takes into account diverse views/beliefs. Offering a reimbursement for titer determinations which show the existing level of antibodies, allowing for more targeted vaccination afterwards.

133593.explanation: Depending on the extra costs for the municipality. It increases opportunities for education and sports, promotes independence, reduces car traffic and CO₂ emissions, accessible to all youth regardless of income.

133534.explanation: CDA wants the municipality to handle public funds carefully. The property tax (OZB) will not rise more than inflation. An increase can only be considered if social services are under pressure. This way we protect what is important to our villages without unnecessarily burdening residents.

133535.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard stands for a fair distribution of costs. Tourists use our facilities and should contribute to them. An increase in tourist tax is therefore possible, as long as it remains reasonable and explainable and does not go too far. This keeps the burden balanced for residents.

133536.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard stands for a strong local economy. Entrepreneurs must be able to start and grow in Krimpenerwaard. We make space for future-proof industrial sites: well accessible, with attention to quality, so they are not grey blocks but fit their environment.

133537.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard gives entrepreneurs space and trust to do business. At the same time, we live together and consider neighbors, safety, and livability. That is why we choose clear frameworks, with tailor-made solutions and agreements in good consultation between entrepreneurs, neighborhood, and municipality.

133538.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard chooses a balance between peace, meeting, and entrepreneurship. The current ordinance offers supermarkets space for shopping Sundays in tourist centers. That arrangement works well and keeps our local economy healthy. Expanding it is unnecessary.

133539.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard stands for careful treatment of our characteristic landscape. A lot of nature has already been created in Krimpenerwaard. Farmers provide food security, landscape, and livability. Therefore, we must protect agricultural land and not convert more to nature.

133540.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard acknowledges the legal responsibility to accommodate status holders. This requires more housing, not shifting scarcity around. Therefore, we focus on new construction and continue to put pressure on the province to prevent different groups from being played against each other.

133542.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard believes that farmers should have the space on their property to build homes for multigenerational living: for children, parents, or informal caregivers. This helps alleviate the housing shortage and keeps families and businesses together. It must be carefully integrated and suitable for the farm.

133545.explanation: CDA sees sports clubs as indispensable for a healthy society. We want to relieve clubs experiencing financial difficulties. This is not always possible through property tax (OZB) but can be done via targeted subsidies. Our focus is on supporting clubs: not the means, but the result matters.

133546.explanation: According to CDA Krimpenerwaard, ensuring safety does not automatically mean hiring more enforcement officers (BOAs). What matters is visible, approachable supervision and good cooperation with the police and community. We are a relatively safe municipality. First, use what we have more smartly; only expand if demonstrably necessary.

133547.explanation: As far as CDA Krimpenerwaard is concerned, coffeeshops do not fit the scale and character of the Krimpenerwaard. We do not want to normalize drug use and choose prevention, livability, and safety. Enforcement and education have priority.

133548.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard sees camera surveillance as a means, not an end in itself. It can help with structural nuisance or insecurity, but only targeted and temporary, and always in consultation with residents and entrepreneurs.

133549.explanation: CDA considers affordable housing crucial. With 34% social rental housing at expansion locations and 30% in infill developments, the standard is already high. Increasing it further makes projects harder to realize. We choose balance and customization, also making room for affordable homeownership for middle incomes and healthy housing turnover.

133551.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard wants to target assistance to those who really need it. Raising it further makes arrangements less focused and ultimately harms the lowest incomes.

133552.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard looks realistically at our municipality. The Krimpenerwaard is expansive and many residents rely on cars. Some towns have a shortage of parking spaces, causing nuisance. Extra parking spaces at appropriate locations are therefore necessary.

133553.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard opts for realistic and workable policy. An environmental zone for trucks in our villages is not feasible and does not solve local problems. The Krimpenerwaard depends on supply and business activities. Sustainability mainly comes from grassroots innovation.

133554.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard supports the transition to electric driving. This requires enough charging points. They must be arranged fairly and smartly: no private spots or preferential treatment in residential streets. Fast charging points mainly belong in larger parking lots and at gas stations.

133555.explanation: Village, neighborhood, and cultural centers are a 'home' for many residents and associations and important for social cohesion. Fortunately, almost every core in the Krimpenerwaard already has such a facility. CDA Krimpenerwaard supports this important provision.

133556.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard is in favor of adequate support for residents with financial problems. We do not see free products provided by the government as a solution. The municipality supports residents through income schemes.

133557.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard stands for a strong local economy. Existing industrial estates should be able to grow with local companies. This way we keep entrepreneurs and jobs in Krimpenerwaard and prevent employment from disappearing elsewhere.

133558.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard is pleased with the additional library branches added in recent years. With nine branches in Krimpenerwaard, we have sufficient libraries.

133559.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard does not consider regular Sunday work for enforcement officers (BOAs) necessary. The police have authority over criminal offenses.

133560.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard wants more involvement of residents in each core. A fixed advisory council in every town sounds good but often leads to extra bureaucracy or disappointment. We prefer targeted, temporary sounding boards per topic or project.

133561.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard is positive but critical about using a citizens' assembly for important decisions. It can be valuable but is no substitute for the municipal council and not a standard tool for every decision.

133562.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard stands for respect and safety for everyone. The municipality raises the rainbow flag on Coming Out Day at the town hall. In villages and cities, the initiative lies with society itself and it is not the municipality's task to organize this everywhere.

133563.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard wants to bring the municipality closer to residents in a way that works. Aldermen have their own portfolios; therefore, we choose contact points per topic. We are not opposed to other forms either. For example, office hours can help to be closer to the communities.

133564.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard values the Silver Museum highly for Schoonhoven. The current municipal subsidy is appropriate and sufficient to keep the museum afloat. An additional increase is not needed. The museum also has its own responsibility to raise funds and income.

133565.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard considers meeting places for youth important, especially when initiatives come from the local community. The municipality should think along and provide space, in good consultation with the surroundings. Together we ensure it fits the core and prevents nuisance.

133567.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard wants vibrant centers with strong hospitality sectors. If financially possible, we reduce or eliminate burdens that unnecessarily complicate entrepreneurship. Terraces contribute to liveliness and social interaction; extra taxes do not help.

133568.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard wants to handle costs carefully for residents, especially around farewells and mourning. Adjustments can only be considered if costs demonstrably change. At this moment, an increase in grave rights is not necessary.

133569.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard chooses to trust entrepreneurs. We do not impose additional local regulations on top of national legislation. We prefer to engage in good dialogues and seek solutions together that work for entrepreneurs and the livability of our cores.

133570.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard believes entrepreneurs are indispensable in making plans. They often know the practical realities well. Therefore, we opt for structural consultation and plans made together with entrepreneurs, so policy becomes better, more realistic, and feasible.

133571.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard favors stable and sustainable shelter. Constantly changing locations or strict deadlines cause unrest and emergency solutions. Good shelter requires continuity, good guidance, and support in the environment to keep shelter humane and manageable.

133572.explanation: CDA supports the transition to electric driving. This requires sufficient charging stations in the public interest. They must be arranged fairly and smartly: no private spots or preferential treatment in residential streets. Fast charging points mainly belong in larger parking lots and at gas stations.

133573.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard sees the sports canteen as a place of encounter. A total alcohol ban goes too far and has counterproductive effects: people go to bars instead, and the club life loses visibility. A healthy society arises from personal responsibility, not strict rules.

133574.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard chooses solidarity: with people fleeing and with other municipalities. We take our responsibility and do our part of the task. That is only possible with good locations, clear agreements about guidance and safety, and attention to support within our cores.

133575.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard wants to allow small-scale wind turbines on farmers’ properties and around businesses, up to about 30 meters, provided they fit well into the landscape. This allows us to generate local sustainable energy without damaging the open polder landscape. A hard limit of 15 meters is restrictive.

133576.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard opposes top-down designation of wind turbines. We discuss height and location through bottom-up conversations with residents and entrepreneurs. Wind energy can help solve grid congestion but only if location, height, and support are well arranged simultaneously.

133577.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard finds greening important for livability and climate. But a fixed target of 10% does not always help. We do not steer by numbers but by effect. Therefore, we choose targeted greening with customization per street and core where it really helps against heat and water nuisance.

133578.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard opposes large-scale solar fields in the open landscape. We choose solar panels on roofs, farms, and industrial estates. This way, we produce sustainable energy without sacrificing valuable landscapes.

133580.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard chooses balance between living and greenery. Only a very small part of the Krimpenerwaard is needed for housing. This way, we can build for young people and families while preserving the vast majority of meadows for meadow birds and the open landscape.

133581.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard wants careful use of space but also acknowledges the housing shortage. Much agricultural land is already owned by project developers and is effectively no longer used agriculturally. It is therefore logical to reallocate that land for housing to provide a place for young people and families.

133582.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard advocates good and fast ambulance care. Establishing an ambulance post is not a direct municipal task. However, we actively work toward better response times throughout the Krimpenerwaard and engage in dialogue with the safety region and care services.

133584.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard focuses on strengthening families. Problems often affect the entire home situation. Family coaches can help, but more money alone is not the solution. We want all involved parties (Local Team, schools, and care) to look beyond the child alone.

133585.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard believes every child should be able to participate at school. That is already supported by the municipality. Through Stichting Leergeld, families up to 130% of the social minimum can request school supplies, including a laptop. This ensures money is not a barrier to education.

133586.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard is positive about affordable and accessible public transport for the elderly but cautious about fully free public transport for all residents aged 67+. We prefer targeted support for elderly who need it and simultaneously invest in good and accessible public transport.

133587.explanation: CDA is not against this considering livability, but expansion costs the municipality a lot of money due to high fuel costs, and sailing at night places a heavy burden on the staff. Moreover, the municipality of Molenlanden does not contribute. If this changes, we will reopen discussions with the ferry service.

133588.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard is cautious about this. Parking garages are expensive and do not fit everywhere with the village character. We prefer smart parking solutions and tailored approaches per core, so that accessibility and livability remain balanced.

133590.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard believes the municipality should assist De Bastille in the search for a new location. However, the ultimate responsibility lies with the youth club itself.

133592.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard holds that vaccination is a personal choice and responsibility. Good and reliable information is important to increase the vaccination rate.

133593.explanation: CDA Krimpenerwaard wants public transport to be good and affordable for young people but is cautious about fully free public transport up to age 18. We prefer targeted support and better public transport connections so young people can travel safely and accessibly to school, sports, and work.

133534.explanation: Disagree, because the municipality’s own expenditures must also be reviewed before adjusting the property tax (OZB) rate.

133535.explanation: Disagree, the increase in the number of tourists visiting the municipality annually, which benefits the local economy, should not be hindered by a significant tax increase.

133536.explanation: Agree, space to undertake business is important. Currently, there is too little room to grow, causing various companies to consider leaving Krimpenerwaard. This harms employment. Existing industrial sites can also still be revitalized.

133537.explanation: Agree, a hospitality entrepreneur should be given space to operate, but without causing nuisance to the surroundings.

133538.explanation: Agree, more space for entrepreneurship, but if an entrepreneur chooses not to open, that is also a choice.

133539.explanation: Agree, there are already 2,250 hectares of nature area under development in Krimpenerwaard.

133540.explanation: Disagree, to keep our cities and villages vital, we believe scarce housing should first be allocated to people from the local municipality.

133542.explanation: Agree, within the building boundary of the existing property.

133545.explanation: The municipality should facilitate sports clubs where necessary.

133546.explanation: Agree, the municipality is already safe, but every incident and violation is one too many and causes a feeling of insecurity. More enforcement officers (BOAs) would allow for more action.

133547.explanation: Agree, regulation is better than prohibition.

133548.explanation: Agree, at locations that residents perceive as unsafe.

133549.explanation: Disagree, it is important for the housing market flow that various types of houses are built; fixing a percentage may result in no construction. Site-specific assessment is needed to determine which homes (owner-occupied and rental) are best suited and in what numbers.

133551.explanation: Disagree, the threshold is already set at 120% of the poverty line.

133552.explanation: Agree, at bottlenecks it must be investigated what is possible to create parking spaces in the vicinity.

133553.explanation: Disagree, but it is important to investigate solutions per village or city to keep as much truck traffic as possible outside the cores for traffic safety reasons.

133554.explanation: Agree, the number should grow with the increase of electric cars and at locations with low charging point density.

133555.explanation: These are costly facilities. Existing facilities must be preserved as much as possible.

133556.explanation: Agree, the municipality should focus on the basic tasks of government.

133557.explanation: Agree, many entrepreneurs indicate that they have no room to expand. This may lead to them moving elsewhere. This costs jobs in the local economy.

133558.explanation: Disagree, the municipality must make an effort to preserve the existing libraries.

133559.explanation: Agree, Sunday is also a day when safety must be guaranteed.

133560.explanation: Neutral, if a village or city wants to organize itself in this way, it can. Participation helps improve municipal plans. However, careful attention must be paid to the representativeness of advisory councils. If there is a need for participation, people can also approach the council factions.

133561.explanation: Neutral, this can be a form in which the municipal council is advised by a group of randomly selected residents. Ultimately, the elected municipal council has the final say.

133562.explanation: The flag is currently raised on this day at the town hall in Schoonhoven. A municipality is neutral in its expressions and serves all residents.

133563.explanation: Agree, the municipal government must be accessible in every core.

133564.explanation: Agree, the museum is unique and has regional and national appeal. The tourism the museum attracts earns back the subsidy in the local economy.

133565.explanation: Agree, there is great need for more nightlife opportunities for young people. Where possible, the municipality should facilitate entrepreneurs.

133567.explanation: Disagree, a terrace tax is an unnecessary tax.

133568.explanation: Agree, but the municipality should not price itself out of the market. If prices are higher than neighboring municipalities, usage will decrease.

133569.explanation: Agree, fewer regulations promote the business climate and innovation.

133570.explanation: Agree, entrepreneurs are naturally creative and practical thinkers. This improves municipal plans.

133571.explanation: We are only in favor of short-term acute emergency shelter. This means as short as possible with a maximum of three years.

133572.explanation: Agree, the number should grow with the increase of electric cars and at locations with low charging point density.

133573.explanation: Disagree, canteens promote togetherness. It is up to the athlete what he/she wants to consume after a match.

133574.explanation: Disagree, despite the legal duty, there must be suitable locations.

133575.explanation: Agree, we are not in favor of large wind turbines in our polder. They spoil the horizon and disturb the meadow birds.

133576.explanation: Agree, we are not in favor of large wind turbines in our polder.

133577.explanation: Agree, this counteracts the paving over of our villages and towns, thereby increasing climate adaptation.

133578.explanation: Disagree, follow the so-called solar ladder. That means that first all possibilities for solar energy on roofs must be used, and then offshore wind energy.

133580.explanation: Agree, building must also take place on the edges of villages and towns to counteract the housing shortage. In this case, the importance of housing outweighs the habitat of meadow birds.

133581.explanation: Disagree, building must also take place on the edges of villages and towns to counteract the housing shortage.

133582.explanation: Agree, rapid medical care and short travel times are vital.

133584.explanation: Disagree, education is primarily the responsibility of parents. Secondly, there are already many help agencies and places that can assist families in difficult situations. Municipal family coaches are not needed on top of that.

133585.explanation: Neutral, several schemes already exist for this.

133586.explanation: Disagree, most elderly people are perfectly capable of paying to use public transport.

133587.explanation: Agree, this makes Schoonhoven more attractive for young people from Nieuwpoort and Groot Ammers.

133588.explanation: Agree, if research shows they are necessary and it is financially feasible.

133590.explanation: Disagree, the youth club initiates, the municipality facilitates.

133592.explanation: Agree, vaccination rates in some areas of our municipality are too low. This poses a danger to everyone, especially young children and the elderly.

133593.explanation: Agree, young people still have too little to spend and are not yet allowed to drive.

133534.explanation: The property tax (OZB) in Krimpenerwaard is low compared to our neighboring municipalities and the rest of the Netherlands. Due to the ravine year, municipal finances are under pressure. Therefore, the income from the OZB must be used where it is needed and not tied to a single purpose.

133535.explanation: Tourists also use our roads, sewage system, and nature. Therefore, it is fair that they make a fair contribution. If we utilize this tourist potential, we can also reduce local charges for our own residents.

133536.explanation: Together we are heading towards a strong, green economy with lots of employment. To achieve this, we focus on green-designed business parks where entrepreneurs benefit from locally generated clean energy. At the same time, it is important to revitalize existing business parks.

133537.explanation: As long as hospitality entrepreneurs consider noise and other disturbances, they are given the freedom to decide when and how late they want to be open.

133538.explanation: Retailers are given the freedom to keep their shops open longer if they wish. Additionally, retailers may choose whether to open their shops on Sundays.

133539.explanation: The distribution of space is unbalanced. We need room for housing, business, energy, nature, and water. As a result, there will be less agricultural land left, but agriculture will not disappear from our polder.

133540.explanation: If social rental housing had simply been built in recent years, the housing shortage would not be so urgent now. We believe that people who need to move quickly should be given priority.

133542.explanation: There are always possibilities to convert barns into homes or to split large farms. Agricultural land is primarily agricultural land. We only change that designation if it is in the public interest.

133545.explanation: Sports clubs provide an important place for meeting and activities. Maintaining these social functions always costs money. If a club is struggling, the municipality must step in, for example by waiving the property tax (OZB).

133546.explanation: It is important that our residents are safe and feel safe. Enforcement officers (BOAs) who are visible and approachable contribute to this. Together with the police, BOAs should also focus on more education at schools about safe traffic participation.

133547.explanation: We can keep cannabis use out of the criminal sphere by regulating it. Coffeeshops are part of that strategy. This prevents street dealing and nuisance and protects minors.

133548.explanation: We want you to feel free and safe in our municipality. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can immediately intervene if something goes wrong. Additionally, we encourage more consultation hours with community police officers.

133549.explanation: In Krimpenerwaard, about 3,000 people are actively looking for social rental housing. At least 200 people respond to every available social rental home. We tackle this waiting list by quickly building more social rental homes. Therefore, new construction plans must contain 35-40% social rental.

133551.explanation: Life is becoming increasingly expensive, and more people, including those who work, are struggling to make ends meet. Therefore, we must expand the group of people entitled to assistance. Additionally, we will expand the range of minimum schemes with, among others, appliance subsidies and the Rotterdam Pass.

133552.explanation: Our public space is limited, so we must make choices. With fewer parking spaces, we can build more homes and preserve space for greenery, playgrounds, and meeting places.

133553.explanation: This measure is more appropriate for (medium) large cities. Moreover, many of our residential areas do not offer the possibility for trucks to "detour."

133554.explanation: Not everyone has a private spot for a charging station. Therefore, the number of public charging points must be increased. Additionally, we need to ensure fast chargers in every residential area.

133555.explanation: Community centers are the beating hearts of our villages and towns. Here you meet others, learn new things, and community spirit arises. Therefore, it is important that every residential area has such a place.

133556.explanation: Every woman and girl must be able to participate in society. Calling in sick at work or school because you cannot afford the necessary menstrual products should not be the solution in this municipality.

133557.explanation: Together we are heading towards a strong, green economy with plenty of employment. To achieve this, we focus on green-designed business parks where entrepreneurs benefit from locally generated clean energy. We will revitalize existing business parks. Expansion is a possibility.

133558.explanation: Not all residential areas have a library. A library is much more than borrowing a book. Especially in a time of increasing polarization and algorithms, it is important that there is a nearby place for everyone for value-free (language) development, media literacy, and opinion formation.

133559.explanation: Our residents deserve a safe living environment every day of the week. Therefore, enforcement officers (BOAs) must also be deployable on Sundays.

133560.explanation: Each community has its own needs and problems. A local advisory council guarantees that those specific signals always reach the municipal government and can never be overlooked.

133561.explanation: Organizing a citizens' assembly with a representative group of our residents is the best way to give our residents control. This allows the community to truly express which direction a sensitive discussion should take.

133562.explanation: Everyone must be able to be themselves in our municipality. Unfortunately, LGBT+ people still often encounter resistance or misunderstanding. By raising the rainbow flag on Coming Out Day, we send a visible message on behalf of all of us that diversity is the norm.

133563.explanation: The most important thing is that every resident of our municipality knows how to find their way to the municipality and that our residents are listened to. If a permanent alderman for each district, who is visible and approachable, can help with this, we welcome that.

133564.explanation: The Silver Museum has already received more funding in recent years. It is now important that the Silver Museum continues to focus on generating its own income and national grants.

133565.explanation: There is depressingly little to do for young people in the Krimpenerwaard. If entrepreneurs need the municipality to help change this, we should seize that opportunity with both hands.

133567.explanation: If an entrepreneur 'borrows' a piece of street or square from the municipality (and therefore from all of us), a reasonable fee may be justified. Of course, a reasonable minimum area should be agreed upon above which the public space tax would be imposed.

133568.explanation: Our residents have the right to a free, secure, and dignified existence. This also includes the end of life. Funerals are expensive, so we must prevent them from becoming unaffordable.

133569.explanation: If we want to continue earning money in the future, we must green our economy. To stimulate this, we want to support our entrepreneurs sustainably with guidance and subsidies to innovate and grow. This way, we ensure that our local economy remains competitive in a rapidly changing world.

133570.explanation: The municipality is there for us. Therefore, it is important that the municipality listens carefully to our needs, whether we are residents, associations, or businesses.

133571.explanation: A lot of money is lost when locations have to be set up and three years later dismantled or repurposed. Not to mention the 'shuffling' of people. It is much more sustainable and humane to use locations for a longer period.

133572.explanation: Not everyone has a private spot for a charging station. Therefore, the number of public charging points must be increased. Additionally, we need to ensure fast chargers in every residential area.

133573.explanation: Sports canteens are important meeting places. A ban undermines the social atmosphere and the much-needed income of associations. We choose prevention and responsibility: good education and enforcement of the age limit are better than a total ban.
